rules.name: Interest Rate Range
rules.description: The note rate must be greater than 0% and not exceed 12%
rules.explanation: Interest rates must be positive and within reasonable market bounds. Loans with 0% or negative rates are typically special programs not eligible for standard pool delivery. Rates exceeding 12% may indicate high-cost loans requiring special handling.
rules.remediation: Verify the interest rate is correctly entered. If the rate is outside normal bounds, check if this loan qualifies for a special program.

provenance.author: system
provenance.changeReason: Initial rule definition based on MortgageMax Guide Section 4301.1

rules.name: Coupon vs Interest Rate
rules.description: The pool coupon rate cannot exceed the loan's interest rate
rules.explanation: The coupon rate is what investors receive. The difference between note rate and coupon (servicing spread) compensates the servicer. If coupon exceeds note rate, servicer takes a loss.
rules.remediation: Correct the coupon rate to be at or below the note rate. Check for data entry errors or incorrect pool assignment.

rules.name: Net Yield Range
rules.description: Net yield must be between 0 and the coupon rate
rules.explanation: Net yield equals coupon minus guarantee fee. Negative yields indicate calculation errors. Yields exceeding coupon suggest the guarantee fee is negative, which is invalid.
rules.remediation: Recalculate the net yield as couponRate minus guaranteeFee. Ensure guarantee fee is a positive value.

rules.name: Positive UPB
rules.description: Unpaid Principal Balance must be greater than $0
rules.explanation: Zero or negative UPB indicates a paid-off or over-paid loan that should not be in active pools.
rules.remediation: Remove paid-off loans from the pool. If UPB should be positive, correct the balance data.

rules.name: Investor Balance vs UPB
rules.description: Current investor balance cannot exceed Unpaid Principal Balance
rules.explanation: The investor can own at most 100% of the loan. Investor balance exceeding UPB suggests amortization calculation errors or incorrect balance updates.
rules.remediation: Verify the investor balance calculation. Check for payment application errors or incorrect principal reductions.

rules.name: Conforming Loan Limit
rules.description: UPB cannot exceed the conforming loan limit
rules.explanation: The 2025 conforming loan limit for most areas is $766,550. High-cost areas have different limits (up to $1,149,825). Loans exceeding limits require jumbo/non-conforming treatment.
rules.remediation: Verify if this is a high-cost area loan. If not, the loan may need to be split or handled as non-conforming.

rules.name: Eligible Property Types
rules.description: Property type must be one of the eligible codes
rules.requirement: propertyType in [SF, CO, CP, PU, MH, 2-4]
rules.explanation: SF=Single Family, CO=Condo, CP=Co-op, PU=PUD, MH=Manufactured Housing, 2-4=Multi-unit. Other property types require special program eligibility.
rules.remediation: Update property type to a valid code, or route to appropriate special program if property type is genuinely different.

rules.name: Active Loan Status
rules.description: Loan status must be Active (A) or Current (C)
rules.explanation: Only active, performing loans are eligible for standard pool delivery. Delinquent, defaulted, or paid-off loans must be excluded.
rules.remediation: Remove non-performing loans from the pool. If status is incorrect, update to reflect current loan performance.

rules.name: Minimum Loan Age
rules.description: Loans must be at least 3 months old (seasoned)
rules.explanation: Seasoning ensures payment history exists and reduces early payment default risk. Very new loans have insufficient track record.
rules.remediation: Hold the loan until it reaches 3 months of age, or document why early delivery is appropriate.

rules.name: Pool Number Assignment
rules.description: Loans must have an assigned pool number for delivery
rules.explanation: Each loan must be assigned to a specific pool before MBS delivery. Unassigned loans cannot be included in pool submissions.
rules.remediation: Assign a valid pool number. Contact your representative if a pool number has not been issued.

rules.name: Valid MBS Prefix
rules.description: MBS pool prefix must be one of the valid codes
rules.explanation: MX=Fixed Rate Pool, MA=Adjustable Rate Pool, MF=Fixed Rate Note. The prefix determines pool type and investor certificate class.
rules.remediation: Assign the correct prefix based on loan characteristics. Fixed-rate loans typically use MX or MF.
